section.heading: Members not on sustained duty: agreements concerning conditions of service

subsection.heading: Agreements.—
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary of the Air Force may enter into a written agreement with a member of the Space Force not on sustained duty—

paragraph.content: requiring the member to serve on active duty for a definite period of time;
specifying the conditions of the member’s service on active duty; and
for a member serving in a space force inactive status, specifying the conditions for the member’s continued service as well as order to active duty with and without the consent of the member.

subsection.heading: Conditions of Service.—
subsection.chapeau: An agreement under subsection (a) shall specify the conditions of service. The Secretary of the Air Force shall prescribe regulations establishing—

paragraph.content: what conditions of service may be specified in the agreement;
the obligations of the parties; and
the consequences of failure to comply with the terms of the agreement.

subsection.heading: Authority for Retention on Active Duty During War or National Emergency.—
subsection.content: If the period of service on active duty of a member under an agreement under subsection (a) expires during a war or during a national emergency declared by Congress or the President, the member concerned may be kept on active duty, without the consent of the member, as otherwise prescribed by law.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 118–31, div. A, title XVII, § 1715, Dec. 22, 2023, 137 Stat. 628.)
